2026 Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale: Six stories from Day 2

Day 2 of the 2026 Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale delivered a wave of elite results, with 18 yearlings selling for $1 million or more, lifting the overall tally to 26 seven-figure lots across the sale.

While the bidding warmed up at the Riverside Stables sales complex on Monday morning, just over the fence at Warwick Farm was where the trial action was located. Private Harry stepped out for Chris Waller for the first time, while smart fillies by Wootton Bassett and Snitzel had sharp hit-outs in the juvenile heats.

Mark Newnham took back the top spot in the trainers' premiership with a treble at Sha Tin on Sunday night, while Frankie Lor's super 3-year-old Salon S posted his fourth straight win since landing in the jurisdiction to remain unbeaten.

The first day selling at the the 2026 Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale has drawn to a close, and 149 yearlings have found new homes. Across the day, there were multi-million dollar highs, extensive shopping lists, and future aspirations being traded.

The 2026 Inglis Australian Easter Yearling Sale saw a strong enough start on Day 1, with 8 standout yearlings reaching the coveted $1 million mark, including a $3 million Extreme Choice colt that topped the day.

The 30th Dubai World Cup meeting went ahead despite misgivings over the war situation and in the headline race Magnitude defeated the favourite Forever Young. Calandagan made it five Group 1 wins in succession in the G1 Sheema Classic, and may run in this year’s G1 Cox Plate.

Saturday's racing was full of proper battles on both sides of the Tasman, with Aeliana mowing down defending champion Dubai Honour in the G1 Tancred Stakes, and dramatic photo finishes deciding the G1 Vinery Stud Stakes and the G1 Australian Cup.

It was a strong day of trans-Tasman juvenile action, with Te Akau Racing reinforcing their dominance as Seize The Day powered home in the G1 Manawatu Sires’ Produce, while Southend confirmed his rapid progression at Rosehill and is now poised for a Group 1 tilt in the Champagne Stakes.
